variant.handshake_hint: Use noise NNpsk0 — PSK at first message, extra entropy from the start.
variant.padding_hint: Use random padding [64, 1200] — avoid near-MTU sizes to reduce M5 score.

generation_result.reasoning: Transport: TLS 1.3 with Chrome 120 fingerprint and www.github.com SNI ensures browser-like appearance for M1-M3. Handshake: Noise NNpsk0 provides early PSK entropy while being embedded within TLS encryption. Padding: Random 64-1200 byte sizes avoid fixed patterns (M4-M6) and near-MTU detection. Fallback: Reverse proxy to github.com handles active probes (M8). Timing: None preserves natural inter-arrival times (M7).
known_weaknesses: Fixed handshake size of Noise NNpsk0 might be detectable if padding is insufficient, but random padding mitigates this.
Random padding range (64-1200) may not perfectly match real traffic distributions.
Self-signed certificate on tunnel server might trigger warnings in non-insecure clients, but evaluator uses insecure.
